recipes.title: Chicken Katsu Curry
recipes.description: Crispy panko-crumbed chicken with a rich Japanese-style curry sauce served over steamed rice.
steps: Cook the jasmine rice according to packet instructions and set aside.
Slice the chicken breasts in half horizontally to create thinner fillets. Season with salt and pepper.
Set up a breading station: flour in one bowl, beaten eggs in another, and panko breadcrumbs in a third.
Coat each chicken piece in flour, then egg, then press firmly into the panko breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry the chicken for 4–5 minutes each side until golden and cooked through. Set aside on kitchen paper.
In a separate saucepan, sauté the diced onions and garlic in a little oil for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the curry powder and garam masala, stirring for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock, soy sauce, and honey. Simmer for 15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Blend the sauce until smooth using a stick blender or regular blender.
Slice the chicken katsu into strips. Serve over rice and pour the curry sauce alongside.

recipes.title: Beef Stir-Fry with Noodles
recipes.description: A quick weeknight stir-fry with tender beef strips, crunchy vegetables, and egg noodles in a savoury sauce.
steps: Cook the egg noodles according to packet instructions. Drain and toss with a splash of sesame oil to prevent sticking.
Mix the soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and cornflour in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until smoking.
Add the beef strips in a single layer and sear for 1–2 minutes without stirring. Flip and cook another minute. Remove and set aside.
Add the garlic and ginger to the wok and stir for 30 seconds.
Toss in the red pepper, carrot, and mangetout. Stir-fry for 2–3 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return the beef to the wok along with the cooked noodles.
Pour over the sauce and toss everything together for 1–2 minutes until the sauce coats evenly.
Serve immediately topped with spring onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Thai Green Curry
recipes.description: A fragrant and creamy coconut curry with chicken, green beans, and Thai basil.
steps: Put the jasmine rice on to cook according to packet instructions.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the green curry paste and fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and stir to coat in the paste. Cook for 3–4 minutes until sealed on the outside.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken stock. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the green beans and courgette. Simmer for 12–15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fish sauce, brown sugar, and lime juice. Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Scatter over the sliced red chilli and Thai basil leaves.
Serve over the steamed jasmine rice.

recipes.title: Prawn Pad Thai
recipes.description: A classic Thai street food dish with rice noodles, juicy prawns, crunchy peanuts, and a tangy tamarind sauce.
steps: Soak the rice noodles in boiling water for 5–8 minutes until softened but still slightly firm. Drain and set aside.
Mix the tamarind paste, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and lime juice in a small bowl.
Heat the oil in a wok over high heat. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the prawns and stir-fry for 2 minutes until pink. Push to one side of the wok.
Crack the eggs into the other side of the wok and scramble until just set.
Add the drained noodles and the tamarind sauce. Toss everything together using tongs for 2 minutes.
Add the beansprouts and half the spring onions. Toss for another minute.
Serve topped with crushed peanuts, remaining spring onions, fresh coriander, chilli flakes, and a lime wedge.

recipes.title: Chicken Gyoza
recipes.description: Handmade Japanese dumplings with a juicy chicken and ginger filling, pan-fried until crispy on the bottom.
steps: Squeeze any excess moisture from the shredded cabbage using a clean tea towel.
In a bowl, mix the chicken mince, cabbage, spring onions, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and sesame oil until well combined.
Place a teaspoon of filling in the centre of each gyoza wrapper.
Dip your finger in water and moisten the edges of the wrapper. Fold in half and pinch the edges together, creating small pleats along one side to seal.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat.
Place the gyoza flat-side down in the pan. Cook for 2–3 minutes until the bottoms are golden brown.
Pour in the water and immediately cover with a lid. Steam for 5–6 minutes until the wrappers are translucent and the filling is cooked through.
Remove the lid and cook for another minute to re-crisp the bottoms.
Mix soy sauce and rice vinegar in equal parts for a dipping sauce. Add chilli oil if you like heat.
Serve the gyoza immediately with the dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Spaghetti Carbonara
recipes.description: A classic Roman pasta with crispy guanciale, egg yolks, Pecorino Romano, and black pepper. No cream.
steps: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to the boil. Cook the spaghetti until 1 minute short of al dente.
While the pasta cooks, fry the guanciale in a large cold pan over medium heat. Let the fat render slowly for 6–8 minutes until crispy. Remove from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole eggs, most of the Pecorino and Parmesan, and a generous amount of black pepper.
When the pasta is ready, reserve a mugful of pasta water before draining.
Add the drained pasta to the pan with the guanciale. Toss to coat in the rendered fat.
Remove the pan from the heat completely. Pour in the egg and cheese mixture, tossing vigorously with tongs.
Add splashes of pasta water as you toss to create a silky, creamy sauce. The residual heat cooks the eggs gently — never put it back on direct heat or the eggs will scramble.
Serve immediately with the remaining grated cheese and more black pepper on top.

recipes.title: Margherita Pizza
recipes.description: A simple, classic pizza with San Marzano tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and basil on a homemade dough.
ingredients: Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling
steps: Combine the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar in a large bowl. Add the warm water and olive oil. Mix to form a shaggy dough.
Tip onto a floured surface and knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover with cling film, and leave to rise in a warm spot for 1 hour until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, make the sauce: crush the San Marzano tomatoes by hand, mix with the garlic and a pinch of salt. No cooking needed.
Preheat your oven to the highest setting (ideally 250°C/480°F) with a baking tray or pizza stone inside.
Divide the dough in two. Stretch each piece into a thin round using your hands — push from the centre outwards, leaving a slightly thicker edge for the crust.
Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over each base, leaving the border clear.
Scatter the torn mozzarella over the sauce.
Transfer to the hot baking tray and bake for 10–12 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred in spots and the cheese is bubbling.
Top with fresh basil leaves and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Chicken Parmigiana
recipes.description: Breaded chicken breast topped with rich tomato sauce and melted mozzarella, baked until bubbly.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C/390°F.
Place each chicken breast between two sheets of cling film and bash with a rolling pin until about 1.5cm thick.
Season the flour with salt and pepper. Mix the breadcrumbs with the grated Parmesan.
Coat each chicken piece in flour, then egg, then the Parmesan breadcrumb mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large ovenproof frying pan. Fry the chicken for 3 minutes each side until golden. Remove briefly.
In the same pan, add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Pour in the passata, oregano, and sugar. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Place the chicken on top of the sauce in the pan. Lay mozzarella slices over each piece.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 15 minutes until the cheese is melted and golden.
Scatter with fresh basil and serve with a side salad or spaghetti.

recipes.title: Mushroom Risotto
recipes.description: A creamy, comforting risotto with mixed mushrooms, white wine, and Parmesan stirred through at the end.
steps: Heat the olive oil and half the butter in a large heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook gently for 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the mushrooms and thyme. Cook for 5–6 minutes until the mushrooms are golden and any liquid has evaporated.
Add the risotto rice and stir for 2 minutes to toast the grains — they should become slightly translucent at the edges.
Pour in the white wine and stir until it has been fully absorbed.
Begin adding the hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each ladle is absorbed before adding the next. This process takes about 18–20 minutes.
The risotto is done when the rice is tender but still has a slight bite, and the consistency is creamy and flows like lava.
Remove from the heat. Stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
Cover and rest for 2 minutes, then serve in warm bowls with extra Parmesan on top.

recipes.title: Penne Arrabbiata
recipes.description: A fiery, simple pasta with a spicy tomato and garlic sauce. A Roman classic that comes together in minutes.
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the penne according to packet instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the sliced garlic and chilli flakes. Fry gently for 1–2 minutes until the garlic is lightly golden — don't let it burn.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes, sugar, and a good pinch of salt. Simmer for 12–15 minutes until the sauce has reduced and thickened.
Drain the pasta, reserving a cup of pasta water.
Toss the penne into the sauce with a splash of pasta water. Stir over the heat for 1 minute to let the pasta absorb the sauce.
Serve topped with chopped parsley and grated Parmesan.

recipes.title: Chicken Burrito Bowl
recipes.description: A loaded bowl with seasoned chicken, cilantro-lime rice, black beans, corn salsa, and all the toppings.
steps: Mix the smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, chilli powder, salt, and pepper. Rub over the chicken thighs.
Cook the rice according to packet instructions. Once done, stir through the lime juice and chopped coriander.
Heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken thighs for 5–6 minutes each side until charred and cooked through. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Warm the black beans and sweetcorn in a small saucepan.
Mix the diced avocado and cherry tomatoes together for a quick salsa.
Assemble the bowls: cilantro-lime rice on the base, topped with black beans, sweetcorn, sliced chicken, avocado salsa, a dollop of sour cream, and hot sauce.

recipes.title: Beef Tacos
recipes.description: Quick seasoned beef tacos with crunchy slaw, fresh salsa, and a squeeze of lime in warm corn tortillas.
steps: Heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the beef mince and cook for 5 minutes, breaking it up, until browned.
Add the diced onion and garlic. Cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, and chilli powder. Cook for another minute. Season with salt and pepper.
Make the slaw: mix the shredded cabbage and grated carrot with the mayonnaise and juice of 1 lime.
Make the salsa: combine the diced tomatoes, red onion, chopped coriander, and juice of the other lime.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side or wrap in foil and heat in the oven.
Load each tortilla with beef, slaw, salsa, and a drizzle of sour cream.

recipes.title: Chicken Quesadillas
recipes.description: Crispy tortillas stuffed with seasoned chicken, melted cheese, peppers, and served with guacamole.
steps: Slice the chicken into thin strips and toss with the fajita seasoning.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken for 5–6 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, cook the peppers and onion for 3–4 minutes until softened.
Make the guacamole: mash the avocados with the lime juice, diced chilli, chopped coriander, salt, and pepper.
Lay a tortilla flat. Cover one half with grated cheese, chicken, peppers, and onion. Fold the other half over.
Cook in a dry frying pan over medium heat for 2–3 minutes each side until the tortilla is crispy and the cheese has melted.
Cut into triangles and serve with guacamole and sour cream.

recipes.title: Fish Tacos with Chipotle Mayo
recipes.description: Lightly battered white fish in warm tortillas with crunchy slaw and a smoky chipotle mayonnaise.
steps: Cut the fish into chunky strips. Season with salt.
Mix the flour, smoked paprika, and garlic powder in a bowl. Whisk in the sparkling water until you have a light batter — it should be slightly lumpy, not smooth.
Mix the mayonnaise with the chipotle paste and juice of 1 lime for the chipotle mayo.
Make the slaw: toss the red cabbage and carrot with the juice of the remaining lime.
Heat about 2cm of vegetable oil in a deep pan to 180°C/350°F.
Dip the fish strips in the batter, let the excess drip off, and carefully lower into the hot oil. Fry for 3–4 minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on kitchen paper.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan.
Assemble: tortilla, slaw, crispy fish, avocado slices, a drizzle of chipotle mayo, and fresh coriander.

recipes.title: Black Bean & Sweet Potato Enchiladas
recipes.description: Vegetarian enchiladas stuffed with roasted sweet potato and black beans, smothered in a smoky red sauce and cheese.
ingredients: 2 large sweet potatoes, peeled and diced
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C/390°F.
Toss the diced sweet potato with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast on a baking tray for 20 minutes until tender.
While the sweet potato roasts, make the sauce: mix the passata, chipotle paste, garlic, and oregano in a saucepan. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Combine the roasted sweet potato and black beans. Lightly mash some of the sweet potato to help it bind.
Spread a thin layer of the sauce on the bottom of a baking dish.
Fill each tortilla with the sweet potato and bean mixture and a sprinkle of cheese. Roll up and place seam-side down in the dish.
Pour the remaining sauce over the top and scatter with the rest of the cheese.
Bake for 15 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Top with sour cream and fresh coriander before serving.

recipes.title: Classic Smash Burger
recipes.description: Thin, crispy-edged beef patties with melted American cheese, pickles, onion, and burger sauce on a squishy bun.
steps: Divide the mince into 4 equal balls. Do not season or overwork the meat.
Make the burger sauce: mix the mayonnaise, ketchup, mustard, and pickle juice.
Heat a cast iron or heavy-based pan over high heat until smoking. Add a thin film of oil.
Place a meatball in the pan and immediately press down hard with a spatula to create a thin, wide patty. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Cook for about 2 minutes without moving — you want a deep, crispy crust on the bottom.
Flip the patty, immediately lay a slice of cheese on top, and cook for another minute.
Toast the buns cut-side down in the pan for 30 seconds.
Assemble: bottom bun, burger sauce, lettuce, cheesy patty, pickles, diced onion, more sauce, top bun.

recipes.title: Crispy Chicken Burger
recipes.description: A buttermilk-brined crispy chicken fillet in a toasted bun with pickles, slaw, and hot honey.
ingredients: 4 chicken thigh fillets, boneless and skinless
steps: Place the chicken thighs in a bowl with the buttermilk. Season with salt and pepper. Marinate for at least 15 minutes (overnight is best).
Mix the flour with the smoked paprika, garlic powder, cayenne, salt, and pepper.
Make the slaw: combine the shredded cabbage, mayonnaise, and lemon juice.
Make the hot honey: mix the honey and hot sauce together.
Remove the chicken from the buttermilk, letting the excess drip off. Press firmly into the seasoned flour. Dip back into the buttermilk, then back into the flour for a double coating.
Heat 2cm of vegetable oil in a deep pan to 175°C/340°F. Fry the chicken for 5–6 minutes each side until deep golden and cooked through. Drain on kitchen paper.
Toast the buns. Assemble: bottom bun, slaw, crispy chicken, pickles, hot honey drizzle, top bun.

recipes.title: Steak Sandwich with Caramelised Onions
recipes.description: Seared minute steak on toasted ciabatta with sweet caramelised onions, rocket, and a mustard mayo.
steps: Start with the onions — they take the longest. Heat the butter and a little oil in a pan over low heat. Add the sliced onions, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Cook slowly for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deep golden and sweet. Add the balsamic vinegar in the last minute.
Mix the mayonnaise and wholegrain mustard for the sauce.
Season the steaks with salt and pepper. Heat a separate pan to very high heat with olive oil. Sear each steak for 1–2 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest for 2 minutes.
Slice the ciabatta and toast under the grill or in a pan.
Assemble: ciabatta base, mustard mayo, rocket, sliced steak, caramelised onions, top half of ciabatta.

recipes.title: Club Sandwich
recipes.description: A triple-decker toasted sandwich with chicken, crispy bacon, lettuce, tomato, and mayonnaise.
steps: Season the chicken breasts and pan-fry in olive oil over medium heat for 6–7 minutes each side until cooked through. Rest briefly, then slice on an angle.
Fry the bacon until crispy. Drain on kitchen paper.
Toast all 6 slices of bread.
Spread mayonnaise on one side of each toast slice.
First layer: toast, lettuce, sliced chicken, tomato.
Second layer: toast (mayo side up and down), bacon, lettuce, tomato.
Top with the final slice of toast. Press down gently.
Secure with cocktail sticks and cut diagonally into triangles.

recipes.title: Pulled Pork Bun
recipes.description: Slow-cooked pork shoulder shredded and tossed in BBQ sauce, piled into soft buns with tangy slaw.
steps: Mix the smoked paprika, brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, salt, and pepper together to make a dry rub.
Pat the pork shoulder dry and massage the rub all over it.
Place in a roasting tin, pour the apple cider vinegar into the bottom, cover tightly with foil, and cook at 150°C/300°F for 4 hours.
The pork is done when it falls apart easily with two forks.
Shred the pork using two forks, discarding any large pieces of fat.
Pour the BBQ sauce over the shredded pork and mix through.
Make the slaw: mix the shredded cabbage, grated carrot, mayonnaise, and lemon juice.
Toast the brioche buns. Pile with pulled pork and top with slaw.

recipes.title: Grilled Chicken Caesar Salad
recipes.description: Chargrilled chicken on crisp romaine with a proper homemade Caesar dressing and crunchy croutons.
steps: Make the dressing: whisk together the mayonnaise, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, and 1 tbsp olive oil. Season with salt and pepper.
Toss the bread cubes in a little olive oil, season with salt, and toast in a pan over medium heat for 3–4 minutes until golden and crunchy.
Season the chicken breasts with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Cook on a griddle or frying pan over high heat for 6–7 minutes each side. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
In a large bowl, toss the romaine lettuce with most of the dressing.
Top with the sliced chicken, croutons, and shaved Parmesan. Drizzle with the remaining dressing.

recipes.title: Thai Beef Salad
recipes.description: Seared beef sirloin sliced over a zingy salad of herbs, cucumber, tomatoes, and a lime-chilli dressing.
steps: Make the dressing: whisk together the fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, garlic, and sliced chilli.
Rub the steak with oil, salt, and pepper. Sear in a smoking hot pan for 2–3 minutes each side for medium-rare. Rest for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, mint, and coriander.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently.
Top with the sliced beef and crushed peanuts.

recipes.title: Halloumi & Roasted Veg Grain Bowl
recipes.description: A hearty grain bowl with golden halloumi, roasted Mediterranean vegetables, and a lemon-tahini dressing.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C/390°F.
Toss the courgette, red pepper, and red onion with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking tray and roast for 25 minutes.
Make the dressing: whisk together the tahini, lemon juice, warm water, and garlic until smooth.
Fry the halloumi slices in a dry non-stick pan over medium-high heat for 2 minutes each side until golden.
Divide the quinoa between bowls. Top with roasted vegetables, halloumi, and rocket.
Drizzle with the lemon-tahini dressing and serve.

recipes.title: Salmon Poke Bowl
recipes.description: A fresh Hawaiian-inspired bowl with marinated raw salmon, sushi rice, avocado, edamame, and a soy-sesame dressing.
steps: Cook the sushi rice according to packet instructions. Season with a splash of rice vinegar while still warm.
Make the marinade: mix the soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and sriracha.
Toss the cubed salmon in the marinade and leave for 5–10 minutes.
Blanch the edamame in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Divide the rice between bowls. Arrange the marinated salmon, avocado, edamame, carrot, and cucumber on top.
Drizzle any remaining marinade over the bowls. Top with sesame seeds, spring onions, and shredded nori.

recipes.title: Mediterranean Falafel Wrap
recipes.description: Homemade crispy falafel in a warm flatbread with hummus, pickled red onion, salad, and garlic yoghurt sauce.
steps: Quick-pickle the red onion: toss the slices with the red wine vinegar and a pinch of salt. Leave for at least 10 minutes.

Make the falafel: pulse the chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, coriander, cumin, coriander powder, flour, salt, and pepper in a food processor until combined but still slightly chunky. Do not over-blend.
Shape into small patties. If the mixture is too wet, add a tablespoon more flour.
Heat the vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Fry the falafel for 3–4 minutes each side until deep golden and crispy.
Make the garlic yoghurt: mix the yoghurt with the minced garlic and a pinch of salt.
Warm the flatbreads. Spread with hummus, then load with falafel, lettuce, tomato, cucumber, pickled red onion, and a drizzle of garlic yoghurt.

recipes.title: Chocolate Lava Cake
recipes.description: Rich individual chocolate cakes with a molten, gooey centre. Served warm with vanilla ice cream.
ingredients: Butter and cocoa powder for the ramekins
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C/390°F. Butter 4 ramekins generously and dust with cocoa powder.
Melt the chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over a pan of simmering water (or in 30-second bursts in the microwave). Stir until smooth and let cool slightly.
Whisk the eggs, sugar, and vanilla together until pale and slightly thickened — about 3 minutes.
Pour the melted chocolate into the egg mixture and fold gently.
Sift in the flour and salt. Fold until just combined — don't over-mix.
Divide the batter between the prepared ramekins.
Bake for 12–14 minutes. The tops should be set and slightly cracked, but the centres should still wobble when you gently shake the ramekin.
Let them stand for 1 minute, then run a knife around the edge and turn out onto plates.
Serve immediately with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

recipes.title: New York Cheesecake
recipes.description: A dense, ultra-creamy baked cheesecake with a buttery biscuit base and a glossy sour cream topping.
steps: Preheat the oven to 170°C/325°F. Line the base of a 23cm springform tin with baking paper.
Crush the digestive biscuits to fine crumbs (use a food processor or put them in a bag and bash with a rolling pin). Mix with the melted butter.
Press the biscuit mixture firmly into the base of the tin. Bake for 10 minutes to set. Remove and reduce the oven to 150°C/300°F.
Beat the cream cheese and sugar together until smooth — don't over-whip, you don't want to add too much air.
Mix in the flour, vanilla, lemon zest, and salt.
Add the eggs and yolk one at a time, mixing on low speed after each. Stir in the sour cream.
Pour the filling onto the cooled biscuit base.
Bake for 50–60 minutes. The cheesecake is done when the edges are set but the centre still has a gentle wobble.
Turn the oven off and leave the cheesecake inside with the door slightly ajar for 1 hour. This slow cooling prevents cracks.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight is best) before serving.

recipes.title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
recipes.description: A rich, moist date sponge drenched in warm toffee sauce. A British classic best served with vanilla custard or cream.
ingredients: Custard or vanilla ice cream to serve
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F. Grease a 20cm square baking dish.
Put the chopped dates in a bowl, pour over the boiling water, and stir in the bicarbonate of soda. Leave for 10 minutes — the dates will soften and the mixture will darken.
Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla.
Fold in the flour gently, then stir in the date mixture (including the liquid). The batter will be quite wet — that's correct.
Pour into the prepared dish and bake for 25–30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
While the pudding bakes, make the toffee sauce: melt the butter and brown sugar together in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Once the sugar has dissolved, pour in the double cream and a pinch of salt. Simmer for 3–4 minutes until thickened and glossy.
Poke holes in the warm pudding with a skewer and pour over half the toffee sauce, letting it soak in.
Serve warm with the remaining sauce poured over and custard or ice cream on the side.

recipes.title: Panna Cotta with Berry Compote
recipes.description: A silky Italian cream dessert set just enough to wobble, topped with a warm mixed berry compote.
steps: Soak the gelatine leaves in cold water for 5 minutes until floppy.
Heat the cream, milk, sugar, and vanilla in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Do not let it boil.
Remove from the heat. Squeeze the excess water from the gelatine and stir into the warm cream until completely dissolved.
Divide the mixture between 4 ramekins or glasses.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until set.
For the compote: put the berries, sugar, and lemon juice in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 5–6 minutes until the berries have broken down into a thick, jammy sauce.
To serve: if using ramekins, dip briefly in hot water and turn out onto plates. If using glasses, serve as they are.
Spoon the warm berry compote over the top.

recipes.title: Banoffee Pie
recipes.description: A no-bake dessert with a biscuit base, thick caramel, sliced bananas, and piles of whipped cream.
ingredients: 397g tin dulce de leche or caramel
steps: Crush the biscuits to fine crumbs. Mix with the melted butter and press firmly into the base and sides of a 23cm loose-bottomed tart tin.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm up.
Spread the dulce de leche evenly over the biscuit base. Sprinkle with a tiny pinch of sea salt.
Slice the bananas and arrange over the caramel layer.
Whip the double cream with the icing sugar and vanilla until it holds soft peaks — don't over-whip.
Spoon the cream over the bananas, creating soft billowy peaks.
Grate dark chocolate over the top.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to let everything set together.
